title: Visa and Mastercard under fire for skyrocketing post-Brexit card fees
text: The Payment Systems Regulator (PSR) has zeroed in on escalating cross-border interchange fees levied by major card operators Mastercard and Visa, proposing a cap on these fees to protect UK businesses from unfair charges. Following an in-depth review, the PSR suggested an initial cap of 0.2% for consumer debit and 0.3% for credit transactions between the UK and European Economic Area (EEA), with a future permanent cap after further analysis.

title: Payments regulator proposes cap on Mastercard, Visa cross-border fees
